---
title: "Research"
---

## Research focus

My research centres on school attendance problems and emotionally based school avoidance, with a broader interest in adolescent mental health, intervention evidence, and system-level measurement.

I am particularly interested in how school disengagement develops across time, how it is defined and measured, and what kinds of interventions appear most effective for students, families, and schools.

## Current research areas

### School attendance problems and emotionally based school avoidance

This work focuses on conceptual clarity, intervention evidence, developmental trajectories, and the institutional representation of attendance difficulties.

### Intervention evidence

A major strand of my current research examines the evidence base for interventions targeting school refusal and emotionally based school avoidance.

**Registered protocol:**  
[PROSPERO CRD420251089918](https://www.crd.york.ac.uk/PROSPERO/view/CRD420251089918)

### Longitudinal and population-level analysis

I am also interested in how school disengagement unfolds across childhood and adolescence, including the role of emotional, behavioural, and contextual risk factors in large-scale longitudinal and administrative datasets.

### Digital mental health and behavioural coping

My broader research interests include problematic social media use, coping processes, and network analytic approaches to psychological phenomena.

## Current projects

### Systematic review of interventions for emotionally based school avoidance

A registered review of intervention approaches for school refusal and emotionally based school avoidance.

### Longitudinal modelling of school disengagement

Work examining developmental patterns of school disengagement using longitudinal cohort data.

### Population-level attendance patterns

Research exploring attendance, non-attendance, and system-level reporting using large education datasets.

## Research approach

My work draws on:

- systematic review methods
- longitudinal modelling
- network analysis
- large-scale administrative and cohort data
- applied and practice-informed perspectives

---
title: "Publications"
---

## Peer-reviewed publications

### 2025

**Conkey-Morrison, C.**, Zarate, D., March, E., Hunt, B., & Stavropoulos, V.  
[Stress-coping mechanisms and problematic social media use: A network analysis and differences across men and women](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2451958825002635)  
*Computers in Human Behavior Reports*.

**Gomez, R., Hunt, B., & Conkey-Morrison, C.**  
[Incremental validity of cognitive impulsivity, emotional impulsivity, and sensation seeking in the predictions of ADHD inattention and hyperactivity/impulsivity symptoms in adults](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10862-025-10252-6)  
*Journal of Psychopathology and Behavioral Assessment*.

**Burleigh, T; Footitt, T; Colder Carras, M; Conkey-Morrison, C; Poulus, D; Stavropoulos, R**
[Flowing in the net of disordered gaming: A network analysis approach](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.abrep.2025.100606)
*Addictive Behaviors Reports*.


### 2024

**Hein, K., Conkey-Morrison, C.**, Burleigh, T. L., Poulus, D., & Stavropoulos, V.  
[Examining how gamers connect with their avatars to assess their anxiety: A novel artificial intelligence approach](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38701623/)  
*Acta Psychologica*.

## Research profiles

- [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0000-1020-2333)
- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ZEvUL_kAAAAJ&hl=en)
- [ResearchGate](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Connor-Conkey-Morrison)
